description: HTTP request dashboard for Java services auto-instrumented by the OpenTelemetry Operator (autoinstrumentation-java:2.26.1). Covers both HTTP server (inbound) and HTTP client (outbound) RED metrics: rate, errors, duration.

panels.description: Total HTTP server (inbound) requests per second across the selected services.
panels.title: Server RPS

panels.description: HTTP server error rate: share of inbound requests tagged with error_type (per OTel semconv, set when response is an error — typically 4xx/5xx depending on framework, and network-level errors).
panels.title: Server Error Rate

panels.description: HTTP server p95 latency across all selected services (aggregated over all routes/methods).
panels.title: Server P95 Latency

panels.description: Total HTTP client (outbound) requests per second made by the selected services.
panels.title: Client RPS

panels.description: HTTP client error rate: share of outbound requests tagged with error_type (includes non-2xx/3xx responses and network/DNS errors).
panels.title: Client Error Rate

panels.description: HTTP client p95 latency across all outbound calls.
panels.title: Client P95 Latency

panels.description: Inbound request rate, stacked by HTTP status code. 2xx green, 3xx blue, 4xx orange, 5xx red in the default color scheme.
panels.title: Server Request Rate by Status Code

panels.description: Inbound latency distribution: p50, p95, p99 from http_server_request_duration_bucket, plus mean from sum/count. p99 reflects tail latency users feel.
panels.title: Server Latency (p50 / p95 / p99 / mean)

panels.description: Percentage of inbound requests flagged as errors (error_type label present). Thresholds: 1% warn, 5% critical.
panels.title: Server Error Rate %

panels.description: p95 latency per HTTP route — helps pinpoint which specific endpoint is slow when the aggregate p95 rises.
panels.title: Server P95 Latency by Route

panels.description: Top 10 routes by traffic (requests/second over the current time range). Sorted descending by RPS.
panels.title: Server — Top Routes by Traffic

panels.description: Top 10 routes by p95 latency over the current time range. Surfaces the slowest endpoints first.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, http_route, http_request_method) (rate(http_server_request_duration_bucket{k8s_namespace_name="$k8s_namespace",service_name=~"$service"}[$__range]))))

panels.title: Server — Top Routes by P95 Latency

panels.description: Inbound request body size distribution (p50/p95/p99) from http_server_request_body_size. Source metric is opt-in in the Java Agent — enable by setting OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_SERVER_EMIT_EXPERIMENTAL_TELEMETRY=true on the instrumented service.
panels.title: Server Request Body Size (p50 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Inbound response body size distribution (p50/p95/p99) from http_server_response_body_size. Requires experimental HTTP server telemetry to be enabled on the Java Agent.
panels.title: Server Response Body Size (p50 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Inbound/outbound bytes-per-second throughput for the HTTP server, computed as the rate of the histogram _sum series. Ingress = bytes received in request bodies; Egress = bytes sent in response bodies.
panels.title: Server Throughput (ingress / egress)

panels.description: p95 response body size broken down by route and method. Large responses on specific routes can pressure network egress, client memory, and serialization cost.
panels.title: Server P95 Response Body Size by Route

panels.description: Outbound request rate broken down by target (server.address:server.port). Reveals which upstream dependencies are being called most.
panels.title: Client Request Rate by Target

panels.description: Outbound latency distribution: p50, p95, p99, mean. Large gap between p50 and p99 indicates tail issues with downstream dependencies.
panels.title: Client Latency (p50 / p95 / p99 / mean)

panels.description: Percentage of outbound calls flagged as errors (error_type set). Spikes typically mean a downstream dependency is degraded.
panels.title: Client Error Rate % by Target

panels.description: p95 outbound latency per target. Quickly identifies which dependency is slow when the aggregate p95 rises.
panels.title: Client P95 Latency by Target

panels.description: Top 10 outbound targets by traffic (RPS over the current time range).
panels.title: Client — Top Targets by Traffic

panels.description: Top 10 outbound targets by p95 latency. Surfaces the slowest downstream dependencies.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, server_address, server_port, http_request_method) (rate(http_client_request_duration_bucket{k8s_namespace_name="$k8s_namespace",service_name=~"$service"}[$__range]))))

panels.title: Client — Top Targets by P95 Latency

title: ACP OpenTelemetry HTTP Requests
